vocab.word: get into the swing of things
vocab.meaning: bắt đầu quen với nhịp độ công việc/hoạt động
vocab.example: It took a few practices, but I eventually got into the swing of things and started scoring goals for the team.

vocab.meaning: làm tròn phần việc của mình, không để người khác gánh hộ
vocab.example: In a volleyball team, everyone has to pull their weight; otherwise, the entire defensive line collapses.

vocab.meaning: tạo ra một môi trường cạnh tranh công bằng
vocab.example: Joining a professional club really leveled the playing field for me, as I finally had access to the same coaching as my peers.

vocab.meaning: nắm rõ cách thức hoạt động/kỹ năng cơ bản
vocab.example: As the captain, I had to help the newcomers who didn't quite know the ropes yet regarding our match strategies.

vocab.meaning: đánh bại đối thủ một cách dễ dàng/áp đảo
vocab.example: During my senior year, our relay team was so well-coordinated that we blew the competition away at the finals.

vocab.meaning: vượt lên chính mình để xử lý tốt một tình huống khó khăn
vocab.example: When our star player got injured, the rest of us had to rise to the occasion to secure the win.

vocab.word: keep one's eye on the ball
vocab.meaning: tập trung cao độ vào mục tiêu chính
vocab.example: Our coach always reminded us to keep our eyes on the ball and not get distracted by the crowd's shouting.

vocab.meaning: một lựa chọn chắc chắn, ít rủi ro
vocab.example: Putting me in as a goalkeeper was usually a safe bet because of my quick reflexes.

vocab.meaning: người/đội dẫn đầu, có khả năng thắng cao nhất
vocab.example: Although we weren't the front runners at the start of the season, we managed to climb to the top of the league.

vocab.word: take the bull by the horns
vocab.meaning: trực diện đối mặt và giải quyết vấn đề khó khăn
vocab.example: I decided to take the bull by the horns and volunteer for the penalty shootout, even though I was incredibly nervous.
